source_jobs.title: Backend Engineer: US & Canada
source_jobs.description:
WE'RE BUILDING A NEW HEALTHCARE CLEARINGHOUSE

Stedi is building the first new healthcare clearinghouse in decades. In the healthcare sector, the Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act of 1996 (HIPAA) requires that all insurance payers exchange transactions such as claims, eligibility checks, prior authorizations, and remittances using a standardized EDI format called X12 HIPAA. Clearinghouses process the majority of these transactions, offering consolidated connectivity to carriers and providers. Until Stedi, the space was occupied entirely by a small group of legacy players, built on outdated, often pre-internet technology.



Stedi is the world's only programmable healthcare clearinghouse. By offering modern API interfaces alongside traditional real-time and batch EDI processes, we enable both healthcare technology businesses and established players to exchange mission-critical transactions. Our clearinghouse product and customer-first approach have set us apart. Stedi was ranked by Ramp https://ramp.com/velocity/top-saas-vendors-on-ramp-march-2026 as one of the fastest-growing SaaS vendors.



We have lightning in a bottle: engineers and designers shipping products week in and week out; a lean business team supporting the company’s infrastructure; passion for automation and eliminating toil; $142 million in funding from top investors like Stripe, Addition, USV, Bloomberg Beta, First Round Capital, and more. To learn more about how we work, watch our founder Zack’s interview with First Round Capital https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=IO6sR-i5rSs.




WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

Stedi is the world's only programmable healthcare clearinghouse. By offering a modern API interface for running eligibility checks, processing claims, and ingesting ERAs, healthcare technology businesses can exchange transactions with payers without dealing with the EDI protocol or carrier-specific connectivity.

As we continue to scale, we are looking for an experienced backend engineer to join our engineering team to build our healthcare APIs and extend existing systems.




HOW WE BUILD

 - We use AWS exclusively for our customer-facing backend infrastructure. We use tools like GitHub, Stripe, and PagerDuty, but all of our application work happens on AWS. 

 - We use serverless technologies almost exclusively: Lambda, API Gateway, SQS, SNS, DynamoDB, Aurora Serverless,  and more. We don’t run a single server.

 - We use CDK (TypeScript) to define infrastructure as code.

 - We write the majority of our API backends and EDI parsing engine in Rust.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Design and develop complex, large-scale web applications and APIs

 - Work closely with AWS to improve our understanding and use of their services

 - Contribute to a culture of engineering excellence and rigor through both coding and writing high-quality, effective communication

 - Build the highest quality, broadest-reaching commercial trading network at the lowest possible price for our customers


WHO YOU ARE 

 - 4+ years of coding experience, previous work in Rust and TypeScript is beneficial

 - 4+ years working with AWS services

 - Committed to working with AWS serverless offerings in a production environment

 - Ability to prioritize your work based on the needs of the business and the customers

 - High bandwidth; thoughtful attention to many areas simultaneously

 - Ability to context switch throughout the course of the day or week as priorities shift

 - Philosophical alignment with the Stedi Standards https://www.stedi.com/careers





We’ve been made aware of individuals impersonating the Stedi recruiting team. Please note:

 - All official communication about roles at Stedi will only come from an @stedi.com http://stedi.com email address, or from our official identification verification partner, Persona, @frompersona.com http://frompersona.com.

 - If you’re unsure whether a message is legitimate or have any concerns, feel free to contact us directly at career

source_jobs.title: Software Development Manager
source_jobs.description:
WE'RE BUILDING A NEW HEALTHCARE CLEARINGHOUSE

Stedi is building the first new healthcare clearinghouse in decades. In the healthcare sector, the Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act of 1996 (HIPAA) requires that all insurance payers exchange transactions such as claims, eligibility checks, prior authorizations, and remittances using a standardized EDI format called X12 HIPAA. Clearinghouses process the majority of these transactions, offering consolidated connectivity to carriers and providers. Until Stedi, the space was occupied entirely by a small group of legacy players, built on outdated, often pre-internet technology.



Stedi is the world's only programmable healthcare clearinghouse. By offering modern API interfaces alongside traditional real-time and batch EDI processes, we enable both healthcare technology businesses and established players to exchange mission-critical transactions. Our clearinghouse product and customer-first approach have set us apart. Stedi was ranked by Ramp https://ramp.com/velocity/top-saas-vendors-on-ramp-march-2026 as one of the fastest-growing SaaS vendors.



We have lightning in a bottle: engineers and designers shipping products week in and week out; a lean business team supporting the company’s infrastructure; passion for automation and eliminating toil; $142 million in funding from top investors like Stripe, Addition, USV, Bloomberg Beta, First Round Capital, and more. To learn more about how we work, watch our founder Zack’s interview with First Round Capital https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=IO6sR-i5rSs.




WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

Stedi is looking for a Software Development Manager to lead a feature team responsible for building highly scalable, resilient, modern APIs, allowing healthcare technology companies and traditional healthcare IT organizations to perform essential transactions for millions of patients each month.

This role offers the opportunity to lead a team of talented engineers, shape the future of a mission-critical part of healthcare infrastructure, and help build the foundational technology that powers modern healthcare systems.




HOW WE BUILD

 - We use AWS exclusively for our customer-facing backend infrastructure. We use tools like GitHub, Stripe, and PagerDuty, but all of our application work happens on AWS. 

 - We use serverless technologies almost exclusively: Lambda, API Gateway, SQS, SNS, DynamoDB, Aurora Serverless, and more. We don’t run a single server.

 - We use CDK (TypeScript) to define infrastructure as code.

 - We write the majority of our API backends and EDI parsing engine in Rust.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Lead a high-performing team to new heights by setting an ambitious pace with rigorous quality expectations.

 - Manage daily engineering workflows, monitoring timelines and resources to ensure projects are executed efficiently and to a high standard.

 - Align engineering deliverables with customer needs, partnering closely with Product and Customer Operations to quickly resolve high-priority issues and ensure timely feature delivery.

 - Increase accountability across the team by setting clear performance expectations and regularly reviewing progress to ensure high standards are consistently met.

 - Identify and implement process enhancements to increase operational efficiency, organizing design reviews, post-mortems, and fostering a culture of continuous improvement.

 - Coordinate operational readiness for incidents and compliance requirements. Oversee run-books, game-day simulations, and ensure security practices are maintained.

 - Mentor engineers, provide guidance, and collaborate with cross-functional teams to promote a culture of technical excellence.




WHO YOU ARE 

 - 5+ years of engineering experience, with at least 3+ years in a management role.

 - 2+ years working with AWS services.

 - Exceptional written communication skills, with the ability to synthesize and clearly convey complex ideas and technical informati

source_jobs.description:

We have lightning in a bottle: engineers and designers shipping products week in and week out; a lean business team supporting the company’s infrastructure; passion for automation and eliminating toil; $142 million in funding from top investors like Stripe, Addition, USV, Bloomberg Beta, First Round Capital, and more. To learn more about how we work, watch our founder Zack’s interview with First Round Capital https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=IO6sR-i5rSs.




WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

Stedi is the world's only programmable healthcare clearinghouse. By offering a modern API interface for running eligibility checks, processing claims, and ingesting ERAs, healthcare technology businesses can exchange transactions with payers without dealing with the EDI protocol or carrier-specific connectivity.

As we continue to scale, we are looking for a Frontend Engineer to bring the Stedi vision to life. You'll work closely with other engineers, designers, and product managers to execute on a broad range of projects – from building foundational features in our web product to testing for reliability and consistency throughout.




HOW WE BUILD

 - Our backend teams use AWS exclusively for all customer-facing backend infrastructure. We use tools like GitHub, Stripe, and PagerDuty, but all the application work happens on AWS.

 - All exposed API endpoints are available to the frontend via strongly-typed SDK clients.

 - Our frontend applications are built using React, Next.js, and TypeScript, based on designs provided by our design team in Figma.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Collaborate with design, product and backend engineering to build web interfaces that power our products and features.

 - Develop reusable, responsive, accessible components using scalable, semantic React components.

 - Contribute to our design language system.

 - Contribute to a culture of engineering excellence and rigor through both coding and writing, as well as high-quality, effective communication




WHO YOU ARE

 - 4+ years of relevant web front-end development experience at a tech-forward software company.

 - Solid understanding of modern frontend technology stack (React, UI testing, CSS-in-JS, Server-side rendering).

 - Advanced knowledge in JavaScript/TypeScript.

 - Experience setting up and maintaining build, test, and deployment tools in production environments.

 - Strong communication skills to bridge the gap between design, product, and engineering.

 - An exceptional contributor who creates and executes their own work.

 - Highly curious; not satisfied with getting something to work, you want to understand why it works and how to make it high-quality.

 - Philosophical alignment with the Stedi Standards https://www.stedi.com/careers.



We’ve been made aware of individuals impersonating the Stedi recruiting team. Please note:

 - All official communicati

We are seeking a product manager to own one of Stedi's products end-to-end. You will help shape requirements, drive prioritization, and work with engineering to execute on the roadmap. This role requires someone who can thrive in ambiguity, translate complex customer needs into actionable product direction, and relentlessly pursue product performance and quality.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Own the product roadmap for substantial portions of our clearinghouse offering.

 - Contribute to a team that ships day in and day out – see our blog https://www.stedi.com/blog and changelog https://www.stedi.com/changelog

 - Report directly to the CEO and work closely with the Head of Engineering to develop product direction.

 - Be the primary author of complex, technical product and business documents (e.g. PR/FAQs, BRDs, GitHub tickets). Work with our healthcare customers to gather feedback and requirements and translate them into actionable product.

 - Work with our design team to develop mockups and wireframes, and our engineering team to prioritize and deliver features.

 - Measure product performance to track adoption and identify areas of investment. Own metrics related to product adoption/engagement.

 - Support and provide expertise on other product initiatives, helping to advance Stedi’s overall product suite.




SUBSTANTIAL PRODUCT MANAGEMENT EXPERIENCE AT A HEALTHCARE SOFTWARE COMPANY, WITH A TRACK RECORD OF DELIVERING SUCCESSFULLY ADOPTED PRODUCTS (B2B PRODUCTS DELIVERED VIA API ARE A MAJOR PLUS).

 - 2+ years working in healthcare revenue cycle management, particularly eligibility verification workflows is strongly preferred.

 - Either Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science or another technical field, or professional experience writing code are strongly preferred.

 - Have an established, demonstrable track record of driving product adoption and managing a product roadmap.

 - Ability to translate complex customer requirements to a highly technical audience.

 - Highly curious; not satisfied with getting something to work, you want to understan

source_jobs.title: Associate General Counsel (Healthtech)
source_jobs.description:
WE'RE BUILDING A NEW HEALTHCARE CLEARINGHOUSE

Stedi is building the first new healthcare clearinghouse in decades. In the healthcare sector, the Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act of 1996 (HIPAA) requires that all insurance payers exchange transactions such as claims, eligibility checks, prior authorizations, and remittances using a standardized EDI format called X12 HIPAA. Clearinghouses process the majority of these transactions, offering consolidated connectivity to carriers and providers. Until Stedi, the space was occupied entirely by a small group of legacy players, built on outdated, often pre-internet technology.



Stedi is the world's only programmable healthcare clearinghouse. By offering modern API interfaces alongside traditional real-time and batch EDI processes, we enable both healthcare technology businesses and established players to exchange mission-critical transactions. Our clearinghouse product and customer-first approach have set us apart. Stedi was ranked by Ramp https://ramp.com/velocity/top-saas-vendors-on-ramp-march-2026 as one of the fastest-growing SaaS vendors.



We have lightning in a bottle: engineers and designers shipping products week in and week out; a lean business team supporting the company’s infrastructure; passion for automation and eliminating toil; $142 million in funding from top investors like Stripe, Addition, USV, Bloomberg Beta, First Round Capital, and more. To learn more about how we work, watch our founder Zack’s interview with First Round Capital https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=IO6sR-i5rSs.




WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

We’re hiring an Associate General Counsel with high potential who is excited to grow with the business. Healthcare experience is strongly preferred – lived experience in the regulatory and commercial complexity of healthcare infrastructure, payers, providers, or healthtech is ideal. As the second attorney on the team, you will get to own a broad variety of complex and critical legal matters for a rapidly growing company in a highly regulated space. You’ll work closely with the Head of Legal and regularly partner with company leadership and teams across the business. You’ll also scale and improve the processes and tools that let a lean team move quickly while operating comfortably within our risk profile. If you bring excellent judgment and work ethic, strong writing, and the ability to translate legal expertise into business strategy, we want to talk – especially if you’re eager to use AI and other technology to raise the quality and speed of the work.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Be a strategic partner across Stedi, using your legal and regulatory expertise to further our business interests, accelerate deals, and remove blockers.

 - Partner closely with the Head of Legal to handle all of Stedi’s legal needs; as the second legal hire for a rapidly growing business with complex and interesting legal work, you’ll have a tremendous opportunity to shape the role.

 - Own customer contracting from first redline through signature, working closely with the Head of Legal to automate all but the most complex edge cases.

 - Drive payer trading partner agreements and key vendor agreements to close quickly and within our risk profile while maintaining our rigorous compliance standards.

 - Serve as Product Counsel for world-class engineers and PMs building innovative offerings in a highly regulated environment.

 - Improve, expand, and automate playbooks and workflows.

 - Take on new areas based on interest and business needs.




WHO YOU ARE

 - You are bar-admitted and ready to take on a role with broad ownership.

 - You have healthcare experience, whether through BigLaw or in-house.

 - You can explain complex issues in plain language, write clearly, and communicate confidently with executives, lawyers, regulators, procurement staff, engineers, and everyone in between.

 - You are comfortable with high-volume work and shifting priorities, and you maintain

source_jobs.title: Payer Integrations Engineer
source_jobs.description:
WE'RE BUILDING A NEW HEALTHCARE CLEARINGHOUSE

Stedi is building the first new healthcare clearinghouse in decades. In the healthcare sector, the Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act of 1996 (HIPAA) requires that all insurance payers exchange transactions such as claims, eligibility checks, prior authorizations, and remittances using a standardized EDI format called X12 HIPAA. Clearinghouses process the majority of these transactions, offering consolidated connectivity to carriers and providers. Until Stedi, the space was occupied entirely by a small group of legacy players, built on outdated, often pre-internet technology.



Stedi is the world's only programmable healthcare clearinghouse. By offering modern API interfaces alongside traditional real-time and batch EDI processes, we enable both healthcare technology businesses and established players to exchange mission-critical transactions. Our clearinghouse product and customer-first approach have set us apart. Stedi was ranked by Ramp https://ramp.com/velocity/top-saas-vendors-on-ramp-march-2026 as one of the fastest-growing SaaS vendors.



We have lightning in a bottle: engineers and designers shipping products week in and week out; a lean business team supporting the company’s infrastructure; passion for automation and eliminating toil; $142 million in funding from top investors like Stripe, Addition, USV, Bloomberg Beta, First Round Capital, and more. To learn more about how we work, watch our founder Zack’s interview with First Round Capital https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=IO6sR-i5rSs.




WHAT WE ARE LOOKING FOR

Stedi is the world's only programmable healthcare clearinghouse. By offering a modern API interface for running eligibility checks, and processing claims, healthcare technology businesses can exchange transactions with payers without dealing with the EDI protocol or carrier-specific connectivity.

Our goal is to increase the number of direct payer integrations that we have by 100x over the next 12 months. This is a complex problem that involves massively parallelizing a process across many payers simultaneously – a process that includes legal agreements, technical implementation and testing, relationship building, and a general do-whatever-it-takes approach to keep the payers moving through the pipeline.

We are looking for a Payer Integrations Engineer to join our growing Payer Strategy & Operations team. You will be the primary technical owner for all integrations that we build directly with payers and other clearinghouses.




WHO YOU ARE

 - You have ample experience integrating with APIs: you are familiar with REST/SOAP APIs and have hands-on experience building integrations to backend systems and applications.

 - You are exceptional at staying on top of many open threads simultaneously. You are hyper-responsive, organized, and thorough. You almost never drop balls.

 - You have experience in customer/externally facing roles: You have experience in customer-facing roles; ideally within technical support, implementation and onboarding, or solutions engineering roles.

 - You have a systems mindset. You go beyond helping with the task at hand. You are able to intuitively understand gaps as you work through implementations and are able to articulate those to both the network operations and engineering team.

 - You do what it takes to get the job done. You are resourceful, self-motivating, self-disciplined, and don’t wait to be told what to do. You put in the hours.

 - You move quickly. We move quickly as an organization. This requires an ability to match our pace and not get lost by responding with urgency (both externally to payers and internally to stakeholders), communicating what you are working on, and proactively asking for help or feedback when you need it.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Lead the technical integration and implementation process with hundreds of payers

 - Establish and continuously improve Stedi’s “payer integration playbo

source_jobs.title: Security Engineer
source_jobs.description:
WE'RE BUILDING A NEW HEALTHCARE CLEARINGHOUSE

Stedi is building the first new healthcare clearinghouse in decades. In the healthcare sector, the Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act of 1996 (HIPAA) requires that all insurance payers exchange transactions such as claims, eligibility checks, prior authorizations, and remittances using a standardized EDI format called X12 HIPAA. Clearinghouses process the majority of these transactions, offering consolidated connectivity to carriers and providers. Until Stedi, the space was occupied entirely by a small group of legacy players, built on outdated, often pre-internet technology.



Stedi is the world's only programmable healthcare clearinghouse. By offering modern API interfaces alongside traditional real-time and batch EDI processes, we enable both healthcare technology businesses and established players to exchange mission-critical transactions. Our clearinghouse product and customer-first approach have set us apart. Stedi was ranked by Ramp https://ramp.com/velocity/top-saas-vendors-on-ramp-march-2026 as one of the fastest-growing SaaS vendors.



We have lightning in a bottle: engineers and designers shipping products week in and week out; a lean business team supporting the company’s infrastructure; passion for automation and eliminating toil; $142 million in funding from top investors like Stripe, Addition, USV, Bloomberg Beta, First Round Capital, and more. To learn more about how we work, watch our founder Zack’s interview with First Round Capital https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=IO6sR-i5rSs.




WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

We are seeking an experienced Security Engineer to join our Platform Team. This team is at the core of our infrastructure, responsible for managing multiple AWS Organizations and providing the foundational tools and services that enable our engineering teams to build reliable, secure, and compliant applications.

The Platform Team’s responsibilities span a wide range of areas, including:

 - The AWS infrastructure that our engineering teams rely on.

 - Management of our GitHub organization and IT operations.

 - Supporting compliance efforts to ensure alignment with industry standards (SOC, HIPAA, HITRUST).

As a Security Engineer, you will play an active role in how we set up our AWS infrastructure, software development lifecycle, and endpoint security. Your contributions will help ensure our engineering teams build applications in a way where it is easy to demonstrate alignment with regulatory and compliance requirements.




HOW WE BUILD

 - We use AWS exclusively for our backend infrastructure that processes customer data. We use tools like GitHub, Stripe, Vanta, and PagerDuty, but all of our application work happens on AWS.

 - We use serverless technologies almost exclusively: Lambda, API Gateway, SQS, SNS, DynamoDB, Aurora Serverless, and more. We don’t run a single server on prem or in the cloud.

 - We use the CDK (TypeScript) to define infrastructure-as-code.

 - We have a strong preference for using AWS products over 3rd party solutions. This simplifies vendor management and compliance, and ensures we can benefit from AWS's integration capabilities and innovations now and in the future.



What you'll do

 - Develop playbooks and address security-related tasks in our AWS serverless environments.

 - Drive improvements in our broader security posture, including application security, endpoint security, access management / just-in-time access, email and web gateways, browser security, and data loss prevention.

 - Collaborate with product engineering teams to raise the bar for security, supporting CI/CD pipelines, dependency management, and secure application design reviews.

 - Help secure and improve our AWS organization using infrastructure as code (CDK), enforcing security controls, and ensuring strong tenant isolation.

 - Continuously assess vulnerabilities and perform regular risk assessments.




WHO YOU ARE

 - 4+ years of exp

source_jobs.title: Head of Security
source_jobs.description:
WE'RE BUILDING A NEW HEALTHCARE CLEARINGHOUSE

Stedi is building the first new healthcare clearinghouse in decades. In the healthcare sector, the Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act of 1996 (HIPAA) requires that all insurance payers exchange transactions such as claims, eligibility checks, prior authorizations, and remittances using a standardized EDI format called X12 HIPAA. Clearinghouses process the majority of these transactions, offering consolidated connectivity to carriers and providers. Until Stedi, the space was occupied entirely by a small group of legacy players, built on outdated, often pre-internet technology.



Stedi is the world's only programmable healthcare clearinghouse. By offering modern API interfaces alongside traditional real-time and batch EDI processes, we enable both healthcare technology businesses and established players to exchange mission-critical transactions. Our clearinghouse product and customer-first approach have set us apart. Stedi was ranked by Ramp https://ramp.com/velocity/top-saas-vendors-on-ramp-march-2026 as one of the fastest-growing SaaS vendors.



We have lightning in a bottle: engineers and designers shipping products week in and week out; a lean business team supporting the company’s infrastructure; passion for automation and eliminating toil; $142 million in funding from top investors like Stripe, Addition, USV, Bloomberg Beta, First Round Capital, and more. To learn more about how we work, watch our founder Zack’s interview with First Round Capital https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=IO6sR-i5rSs.




WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

We are hiring a Head of Security to take full ownership of security at Stedi, reporting directly to the CEO and working at the intersection of engineering, legal, product, and more.

At Stedi, security is job zero. There is nothing more important than securing our systems. This role exists to operationalize that principle across every function of the company.

You won’t be building from scratch. We already have SOC 2 Type 2 and HIPAA certifications and will soon have HITRUST R2 certification. We view these compliance items as a baseline starting point and not the final destination. We have invested heavily in security from the earliest days. We have extensive controls across our engineering and IT infrastructure (from SCPs to DLP and everything in between), and 100% of our customer data is processed within AWS without exception. We work extensively with AWS’s native tools as well as with AWS teams, including on an IAM access vulnerability that we discovered https://www.stedi.com/blog/stedi-discovered-an-aws-access-vulnerability.

You will own our security function end-to-end: incident readiness, regulatory obligations, customer trust, and the day-to-day fundamentals that enable everything else. You will be the bridge between engineering and legal, working closely with leadership from both teams and the CEO. You’ll inherit a strong foundation to scale in our next phase of growth – building out the team, programs, and processes that let a lean company move fast while maintaining a world-class security posture.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Own and build Stedi's security program end-to-end, including policies, controls, procedures, security tooling, training, vulnerability management, vendor risk, and more.

 - Be a strong hands-on contributor from day 1 while also building a roadmap for scaling the security function as the company continues to grow. We have a culture where leaders are contributors and are deeply involved in the technical details.

 - Advise on security risk tied to product decisions, architecture, and partnerships.

 - Leverage our best-in-category security posture to unlock new customers and strategic relationships.

 - Partner with Engineering to maintain security excellence while minimizing development friction.

 - Lead breach preparedness and incident response: build, test, and own the Security Incident Response Plan, Disaster Reco

source_jobs.title: General Counsel
source_jobs.description:
WE'RE BUILDING A NEW HEALTHCARE CLEARINGHOUSE

Stedi is building the first new healthcare clearinghouse in decades. In the healthcare sector, the Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act of 1996 (HIPAA) requires that all insurance payers exchange transactions such as claims, eligibility checks, prior authorizations, and remittances using a standardized EDI format called X12 HIPAA. Clearinghouses process the majority of these transactions, offering consolidated connectivity to carriers and providers. Until Stedi, the space was occupied entirely by a small group of legacy players, built on outdated, often pre-internet technology.



Stedi is the world's only programmable healthcare clearinghouse. By offering modern API interfaces alongside traditional real-time and batch EDI processes, we enable both healthcare technology businesses and established players to exchange mission-critical transactions. Our clearinghouse product and customer-first approach have set us apart. Stedi was ranked by Ramp https://ramp.com/velocity/top-saas-vendors-on-ramp-march-2026 as one of the fastest-growing SaaS vendors.



We have lightning in a bottle: engineers and designers shipping products week in and week out; a lean business team supporting the company’s infrastructure; passion for automation and eliminating toil; $142 million in funding from top investors like Stripe, Addition, USV, Bloomberg Beta, First Round Capital, and more. To learn more about how we work, watch our founder Zack’s interview with First Round Capital https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=IO6sR-i5rSs.




WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

As Stedi continues to scale rapidly, we're competing against legacy players whose defense has been centered on complexity and opacity. We're winning, and our growth is creating legal and regulatory questions where precedent is limited and a standard approach doesn’t always exist.

We're looking for a General Counsel to own the questions that arise in this context, turning regulatory uncertainty into a competitive advantage and novel products into defensible, differentiated offerings. The General Counsel will also shape our defensive and offensive legal strategies – not just manage risk, but actively guide Stedi as it defends its interests and advances its positions. In addition, the role covers Stedi’s full legal function, working in close partnership with our Head of Legal on everything from commercial agreements and disputes to compliance, IP, employment, corporate governance, and fundraising.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Develop and execute the regulatory and legal strategy to deepen Stedi's relationships with payers, navigating enforcement, compliance requirements, and relationship dynamics with organizations that control access to the market.

 - Lead competitive and enforcement matters, defending and advancing Stedi’s interests, where legal strategy directly determines market outcomes.

 - Build the legal framework for novel data products that operate in complex regulatory areas, turning uncertainty into defensible product strategy.

 - Use legal and regulatory expertise to identify growth opportunities, accelerate deals, and remove blockers.

 - Own the full scope of legal for a fast-growing company alongside our Head of Legal, including contracts, disputes, compliance, IP, employment, corporate governance, and fundraising.

 - Partner directly with the CEO and board on strategic decisions where legal and regulatory considerations shape the business direction.




WHO YOU ARE

 - 10+ years of relevant experience between law firms and fast-paced in-house roles.

 - Significant in-house experience, including managing litigations and other disputes, ideally while in a senior legal role at a high-growth company.

 - Deep healthcare experience is strongly preferred. Lived experience in the regulatory and commercial complexity of healthcare infrastructure, payers, providers, or health tech is ideal. We'll also consider deep experience in another hea

source_jobs.title: Product Designer
source_jobs.description:
WE'RE BUILDING A NEW HEALTHCARE CLEARINGHOUSE

Stedi is building the first new healthcare clearinghouse in decades. In the healthcare sector, the Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act of 1996 (HIPAA) requires that all insurance payers exchange transactions such as claims, eligibility checks, prior authorizations, and remittances using a standardized EDI format called X12 HIPAA. Clearinghouses process the majority of these transactions, offering consolidated connectivity to carriers and providers. Until Stedi, the space was occupied entirely by a small group of legacy players, built on outdated, often pre-internet technology.



Stedi is the world's only programmable healthcare clearinghouse. By offering modern API interfaces alongside traditional real-time and batch EDI processes, we enable both healthcare technology businesses and established players to exchange mission-critical transactions. Our clearinghouse product and customer-first approach have set us apart. Stedi was ranked by Ramp https://ramp.com/velocity/top-saas-vendors-on-ramp-march-2026 as one of the fastest-growing SaaS vendors.



We have lightning in a bottle: engineers and designers shipping products week in and week out; a lean business team supporting the company’s infrastructure; passion for automation and eliminating toil; $142 million in funding from top investors like Stripe, Addition, USV, Bloomberg Beta, First Round Capital, and more. To learn more about how we work, watch our founder Zack’s interview with First Round Capital https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=IO6sR-i5rSs.




WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

Stedi is the world's only programmable healthcare clearinghouse. By offering a modern API interface for running eligibility checks and processing claims, healthcare technology businesses can exchange transactions with payers without dealing with the EDI protocol or carrier-specific connectivity.

As we continue to scale, we are looking for an experienced product designer who can own the look, feel, polish, and implementation of the UI of our new healthcare products. We work in a highly technical space—it’s full of detail, interesting interaction problems, and knotty user experience challenges. If you’re passionate about healthcare and mastering the design of complex systems, we’d love to hear from you.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Deliver high-quality design work, from conception to delivery, for various products on the Stedi platform

 - Work collaboratively and build with engineers and our customer team to identify and solve user, product, and business problems

 - Help define strategy — understanding and articulating the product’s goals, success metrics, roadmap, and commitments

 - Design and prototype multiple ideas quickly and connect regularly with customers to validate product directions

 - Improve our internal design system, and push for higher quality across the whole product, not just the screens in front of you.


WHO YOU ARE

 - 4+ years of product design experience at a product-driven tech company shipping software

 - Healthcare experience required

 - Frontend development experience with React, Next.js and HTML/CSS

 - Experience shipping software that works on complex problems, such as developer tools, revenue-cycle management, fintech systems, process management, or others

 - Have good practical knowledge of end-to-end product design — from user research through high-fidelity design and developer handoff.

 - Comfortable working with engineering, design, and business teams

 - A great juggler of creative optimism and critical investigation

 - Comfortable building iteratively to gather feedback

 - Located in PST - EST time zones.

 - Philosophical alignment with the Stedi Standards. https://www.stedi.com/careers



We’ve been made aware of individuals impersonating the Stedi recruiting team. Please note:

 - All official communication about roles at Stedi will only come from an @stedi.com http://stedi.com email address, or from o

notes: Healthcare clearinghouse with strong engineering focus
